query_analysis:
Summary of the Query:
The query asks for the company responsible for the largest pharmaceutical settlement.

Skills and Tools Needed:
- Research Skills: To gather and analyze information about pharmaceutical settlements.
- Data Analysis Skills: To compare the sizes of different settlements.
- Web Search Skills: To find reliable sources of information about pharmaceutical settlements.

Explanation of Skills and Tools:
1. Research Skills:
   - Required to understand the context and nature of pharmaceutical settlements.
   - Helps in identifying credible sources and relevant information.

2. Data Analysis Skills:
   - Needed to compare the sizes of different settlements.
   - Helps in determining which company has the largest settlement.

3. Web Search Skills:
   - Essential for locating and verifying information about pharmaceutical settlements.
   - Can be used to find articles, news reports, and official documents related to settlements.

Tools:
- **Ground_Google_Search_Tool**:
   - Useful for conducting web searches to find articles, news reports, and official documents about pharmaceutical settlements.
   - Provides real-time information and citations.

- **Wikipedia_RAG_Search_Tool**:
   - Helps in finding comprehensive information about pharmaceutical settlements directly from Wikipedia.
   - Provides relevant pages, abstracts, and URLs for further research.

- **Web_RAG_Search_Tool**:
   - Can be used to retrieve specific information from websites that are not easily accessible through the other tools.
   - Useful if the information is contained in less structured or less accessible websites.

Additional Considerations:
- Verify the credibility of the sources used to ensure the accuracy of the information.
- Consider multiple sources and cross-reference findings to build a robust understanding.
- The information may be subject to change over time, so consider the date of the information when evaluating its relevance.

- **Memory (Tools Used & Results):** {'Action Step 1': {'tool_name': 'Ground_Google_Search_Tool', 'sub_goal': '- Find reliable sources and information about the largest pharmaceutical settlement and the company responsible for it.', 'command': 'execution = tool.execute(query="Which company was responsible for the largest pharmaceutical settlement?")', 'result': ["The largest pharmaceutical settlement attributed to a single company was by Purdue Pharma, with a $7.4 billion settlement related to the opioid crisis[1](https://www.enjuris.com/blog/resources/largest-pharmaceutical-settlements-lawsuits/). This settlement was reached with members of the Sackler family and their company, Purdue Pharma Inc., and aimed to address the opioid crisis, also barring the Sackler family from selling opioids in the United States[1](https://www.enjuris.com/blog/resources/largest-pharmaceutical-settlements-lawsuits/).\n\nPrior to this, GlaxoSmithKline (GSK) was responsible for what was considered the largest pharmaceutical lawsuit in U.S. history at the time, settling for $3 billion in 2012[2](https://www.angeloslaw.com/blog/2022/01/10-biggest-pharmaceutical-settlements/), [3](https://downloads.regulations.gov/FDA-2021-P-0460-13033/attachment_4.pdf), [4](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_largest_pharmaceutical_settlements). This settlement stemmed from GSK pleading guilty to promoting two drugs for unapproved uses and failing to report safety data for a diabetes drug[2](https://www.angeloslaw.com/blog/2022/01/10-biggest-pharmaceutical-settlements/), [3](https://downloads.regulations.gov/FDA-2021-P-0460-13033/attachment_4.pdf).\n\nOther significant settlements include:\n*   Pfizer's $2.3 billion settlement in 2009 for unlawful prescription drug promotions[2](https://www.angeloslaw.com/blog/2022/01/10-biggest-pharmaceutical-settlements/), [5](https://www.oal-law.com/blog/5-largest-pharmaceutical-lawsuits/), [3](https://downloads.regulations.gov/FDA-2021-P-0460-13033/attachment_4.pdf).\n*   Johnson & Johnson's $2.2 billion settlement in 2013 for misbranding the antipsychotic drug Risperdal, among other allegations[2](https://www.angeloslaw.com/blog/2022/01/10-biggest-pharmaceutical-settlements/), [5](https://www.oal-law.com/blog/5-largest-pharmaceutical-lawsuits/), [3](https://downloads.regulations.gov/FDA-2021-P-0460-13033/attachment_4.pdf).\n*   Eli Lilly and Company's $1.4 billion settlement for off-label marketing of its antipsychotic drug Zyprexa[2](https://www.angeloslaw.com/blog/2022/01/10-biggest-pharmaceutical-settlements/), [1](https://www.enjuris.com/blog/resources/largest-pharmaceutical-settlements-lawsuits/).\n*   Abbott Laboratories' $1.6 billion settlement in 2012 for unlawful promotion of the prescription drug Depakote[2](https://www.angeloslaw.com/blog/2022/01/10-biggest-pharmaceutical-settlements/), [5](https://www.oal-law.com/blog/5-largest-pharmaceutical-lawsuits/), [3](https://downloads.regulations.gov/FDA-2021-P-0460-13033/attachment_4.pdf).\n\nWhile there was a larger $26 billion settlement in 2022 involving multiple companies (Cardinal Health, McKesson, AmerisourceBergen, and Johnson & Johnson) to fund efforts to combat the opioid crisis, this was a multi-party agreement rather than a settlement from a single company[5](https://www.oal-law.com/blog/5-largest-pharmaceutical-lawsuits/)."]}}

Conclusion: STOP

The memory contains detailed information about the largest pharmaceutical settlement, specifically mentioning that Purdue Pharma was responsible for the largest single company settlement of $7.4 billion related to the opioid crisis. This information directly answers the query and does not require additional tools to be more accurate or complete.

Action Step 1.sub_goal: - Find reliable sources and information about the largest pharmaceutical settlement and the company responsible for it.
Action Step 1.command: execution = tool.execute(query="Which company was responsible for the largest pharmaceutical settlement?")
result:
The largest pharmaceutical settlement attributed to a single company was by Purdue Pharma, with a $7.4 billion settlement related to the opioid crisis[1](https://www.enjuris.com/blog/resources/largest-pharmaceutical-settlements-lawsuits/). This settlement was reached with members of the Sackler family and their company, Purdue Pharma Inc., and aimed to address the opioid crisis, also barring the Sackler family from selling opioids in the United States[1](https://www.enjuris.com/blog/resources/largest-pharmaceutical-settlements-lawsuits/).

Prior to this, GlaxoSmithKline (GSK) was responsible for what was considered the largest pharmaceutical lawsuit in U.S. history at the time, settling for $3 billion in 2012[2](https://www.angeloslaw.com/blog/2022/01/10-biggest-pharmaceutical-settlements/), [3](https://downloads.regulations.gov/FDA-2021-P-0460-13033/attachment_4.pdf), [4](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_largest_pharmaceutical_settlements). This settlement stemmed from GSK pleading guilty to promoting two drugs for unapproved uses and failing to report safety data for a diabetes drug[2](https://www.angeloslaw.com/blog/2022/01/10-biggest-pharmaceutical-settlements/), [3](https://downloads.regulations.gov/FDA-2021-P-0460-13033/attachment_4.pdf).

Other significant settlements include:
*   Pfizer's $2.3 billion settlement in 2009 for unlawful prescription drug promotions[2](https://www.angeloslaw.com/blog/2022/01/10-biggest-pharmaceutical-settlements/), [5](https://www.oal-law.com/blog/5-largest-pharmaceutical-lawsuits/), [3](https://downloads.regulations.gov/FDA-2021-P-0460-13033/attachment_4.pdf).
*   Johnson & Johnson's $2.2 billion settlement in 2013 for misbranding the antipsychotic drug Risperdal, among other allegations[2](https://www.angeloslaw.com/blog/2022/01/10-biggest-pharmaceutical-settlements/), [5](https://www.oal-law.com/blog/5-largest-pharmaceutical-lawsuits/), [3](https://downloads.regulations.gov/FDA-2021-P-0460-13033/attachment_4.pdf).
*   Eli Lilly and Company's $1.4 billion settlement for off-label marketing of its antipsychotic drug Zyprexa[2](https://www.angeloslaw.com/blog/2022/01/10-biggest-pharmaceutical-settlements/), [1](https://www.enjuris.com/blog/resources/largest-pharmaceutical-settlements-lawsuits/).
*   Abbott Laboratories' $1.6 billion settlement in 2012 for unlawful promotion of the prescription drug Depakote[2](https://www.angeloslaw.com/blog/2022/01/10-biggest-pharmaceutical-settlements/), [5](https://www.oal-law.com/blog/5-largest-pharmaceutical-lawsuits/), [3](https://downloads.regulations.gov/FDA-2021-P-0460-13033/attachment_4.pdf).

While there was a larger $26 billion settlement in 2022 involving multiple companies (Cardinal Health, McKesson, AmerisourceBergen, and Johnson & Johnson) to fund efforts to combat the opioid crisis, this was a multi-party agreement rather than a settlement from a single company[5](https://www.oal-law.com/blog/5-largest-pharmaceutical-lawsuits/).

direct_output:
<answer>
The largest pharmaceutical settlement attributed to a single company was by Purdue Pharma, with a $7.4 billion settlement related to the opioid crisis. This settlement was reached with members of the Sackler family and their company, Purdue Pharma Inc., and aimed to address the opioid crisis, also barring the Sackler family from selling opioids in the United States.
</answer>
